one_time_description: Government of Canada - IR 27B Settlement of $3,746,000 recognized as non-recurring settlement revenue
economic_notes: Lytton Creek wildfire on June 30, 2021 destroyed 5 band buildings and 39 houses on three reserves, band office destroyed. Village of Lytton had 90% of homes and businesses destroyed. Significant ongoing reconstruction. Government business enterprises include Kumcheen Enterprises Ltd., Lytton Development Corp., Tlkemcheen Economic Development LLP, and Lytton First Nation Economic Development Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Prior year (2022) audit was qualified due to Lytton Creek wildfire documentation issues. Current year audit is unqualified. Four government business enterprises accounted for on modified equity basis. Social Development and Health are combined as one segment in statements. IR 27B Settlement of $3,746,000 is a likely one-time item. Significant capital spending on wildfire reconstruction. Stein Valley Nlakapamux School Society appears in accounts payable at $2,720,440. No schedule of remuneration provided. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $39,214,672 + FNHA $1,565,328 + CMHC $144,271 + Province of BC $1,110,447 + Dept of Justice $142,316 + Community Partners Legal Aid $21,600 + FNESC $15,511 + Govt of Canada IAFSA-026 $476,072 + IR 27B Settlement $3,746,000 - deferred revenue adjustment not applied to individual lines.
